statement: The kanji 一 means 'one, one radical (no.1)'
explanation: A pictograph of a one line.
If you flip it upside-down and left-to-right it looks like a seven.
It's only ten thousand yen for a clothes hanger which you can attach to the ceiling by a rope!
A pictograph of three lines.

A pictograph of a magical divining rod hovering above the ground. Do not confuse it with 下, which also features a magical levitating divining rod, however that one is crooked, so it's incorrectly hove

A pictograph of a crooked divining rod hovering in the air right below the ceiling. Do not confuse it with 上, which also features a magical levitating divining rod, however that one is not crooked, so
A pictograph of a stick jammed in someone's mouth. Maybe a prehistoric dentist keeping his patient's mouth open...?
A pictograph of two lines.
It has five lines. It looks like a 5. It's a five.
A pictograph of a headless, armless person.

You hear an evil laughter from below. Fufufu. Crap! It's getting closer and closer! But where should you go? You're trapped here and you can only go down. There is one way — now is the time to go up o

statement: The kanji 休 means 'rest, day off'
explanation: A really thin person is not going to be very strong physically, so when covering long distances he'll have to rest quite frequenty. And what's the best place to rest when you're out in the nature? Wel
Imagine a really thin person lifting up a car. What?! Even such a thin person can do that?! It's a real thing actually, it's called hysterical strength.
It's a cow with human legs! Wow! I've never seen such a creature before!
A pictograph of a person with one hand over his head pointing to the entrance on the left. Over there, enter over there!

A circular one yen coin. This character looks like one of those bank teller windows where you can obtain such coins, with the upper two rectangles being the glass windows through which you can see the
You see two mountains stacked on top of each other that stand in your way. Uh... is that the exit...? Why is getting out of here so hard?!

statement: The kanji 前 means 'in front, before'
explanation: If you're going to cut off someone's leg as a punishment in a gruesome ritual then you might as well do that in front of a creepy pair of antlers on a wall, right?
A pictograph of a bird's eye view of a road leading north.
A pictograph of a cross, which signifies the number ten. You know, like the ten commandments?
An extra stroke on the top, and a ten becomes a thousand! Why? Well, three strokes, three zeroes!

statement: The kanji 午 means 'noon, sign of the horse'
explanation: It's a pictograph of a cow, but it's really close to the fence, with its head reaching over the fence. It's looking an you and saying "noon, noon". Huh, it looks like this cow doesn't moo like every o
A pictograph of a part of a fence that will be destroyed. There are three boards to smash, the first one is already in half and there are still two more to go
If someone's once again by your side when your in trouble for the millionth time, you know you have a true friend.

When you're having a date this evening with a girl you've anonymously met online it'd be the best to open your mouth and tell her your name before the sun sets and you'll go to your apartment to do th
The country of Japan's flag looks like a red ball inside of a white rectangular box.

A pictograph of a cross planted into the earth. The lower stroke is longer since it signifies the ground, or the horizon, which infinitely stretches in every direction. Do not confuse with 士 where the
When you want to test out a new sword it's best to grab a stick, stick it into the ground, and slash it with your new sword during the evening while it's still bright outside.
A pictograph of a person with their hands stretched out. Why is he doing that? Who knows. He's really large, so it's not like you can do anything about him; he'll beat you up anyway.
A pictograph of a large man with outstretched arms, and one line above his head which represents the heaven, which is a plane of existence one level higher that the one the man's currently in. Do not
This kanji can be decomposed into three strokes:  "く" ("ku" in hiragana), "ノ" ("no" in katakana), "一" ("ichi" in kanji),  which gives us くノ一 ("kunoichi") — a female ninja.
A pictograph of a child. He's wearing a bright red Mario cap, and he has his arms outstretched to give you a hug. So cute!

What do you do when your child just won't behave and absolutely refuses to study? You lock him up in a derelict hut deep in the forest, and force him to study under a roof full of tiny hairs which are
We see here a pair of short legs and a fishing hook between them. Yes, that must be a pretty small fisherman if his fishing hook is longer than his legs!
A pictograph of a mountain.
A pictograph of a bird's eye view of a river.
Spending almost a year making an elaborate fake cow head — now that's basically a whole year wasted. What are you going to use that fake cow head for anyway, huh?
It's going to be a late winter this year. The only winter clothes you have cannot be even called clothes anymore; they are so tattered that they are literally falling apart. Quickly, grab your thread

A pictograph of a sun, which makes a day. In the ancient times this character was once written as a circle with a dot inside, but over time it became rectangular and the dot in the middle morphed into

Imagine a Buddhist temple, late in the day, with the sun already almost setting in the west. The monks at the temple are busy meditating, with the sun's gentle warmth caressing their faces. They've be
In the old days if you wanted to write something you'd use your brush and some ink on a piece of parchment, and then lay it out in the sun to dry.
A pictograph of a moon, whose full cycle takes approximately a month. In the ancient times this character looked more like a crescent moon, but over time it morphed into being drawn like this.
A pictograph of a tree, any kind of a tree.
A pictograph of a tree with an extra stroke at the bottom to show where it's supposed to be cut. Because you first need to cut at least one tree to make a book!
One serving of rice, coming up!
A pictograph of a rising sun behind a tree, representing east.
A school is not only a place where you learn; it's also a place where you mingle with your classmates around a tree in the courtyard during the breaks.
A pictograph of woman's breasts with nipples, turned on its side.
My mother dresses up every Halloween as a cow, she even wears a cow head.
If you inhale a lot of nasty vapors you'll collapse to the floor, your eyes will turn into crosses like in a cartoon (X_X) and you'll lose your current state of mind.
A pictograph of a bird's eye view of water in a river and two of its banks on each side.
A pictograph of a person on fire.

Traditionally when it's time to punish a child it's usually the father who doles out the punishment. And how to better punish a child than to have him sit his short legs on bare folding chair legs? Fo
That decapitated cow head you have over there will eventually return to the earth, be slowly absorbed, and will be transformed into nutrients that will nurture new life. Ah, the cycle of life.
A man is someone who has the power to work in a rice field.

We are very fortunate that the Sun is approximately 150 million kilometres apart from us. If you'd take only a single drop of the Sun, and put it here on Earth, it'd be so bright you'd see nothing but
Stick your ear out of the gate and you'll hear everything that goes on outside.
Every journey starts the same: first you take a single small step and stop standing still, and then you go!
A walking eye with legs attached; surely its main purpose is to see.
You can say many things without speaking 一 with your hands, your body language, or through your actions, but to speak, ah, yes, you need to actually use your tongue to say the things you want to say.
Five mouths are saying something to each other in a language I can't understand. Russian spies perhaps...?
Every good salesman that tries to sell you something will say whatever they can to get you to buy their product, but don't be fooled! You need to read the fine-print! There's always a catch.
A pictograph of a vehicle, show from a bird's-eye view. You have the front wheels at the top, the back wheels at the bottom, and the middle is where you put stuff in, be it people or goods.
Money and gold is known to make people corrupt. Even if it's not obvious, even if someone doesn't have a pair of horns growing out of their head and instead they're hiding those horns in their pants,

A hair comb with really long legs (also called "teeth" — you know, the part which you actually use to brush your hair with?) is a fashion accessory that's not going to be seen often. It may be long, b
There is only a limited interval of time during which the sun can be seen through the gate.
A pictograph of a window through which you can see the rain.
An umbrella can protect you from the sun and from the rain, but it's not very effective against an electrical storm. If you get struck by a lightning it's over for you.
A good person will eat everything that their momma puts on their plate.
